Name: Squid Security Update Advisory (SQUID-2020:4)
description: Squid is prone to multiple vulnerabilities in the HTTP Digest authentication.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Aected Software/OS Squid versions 2.x, 3.x through 3.5.28, 4.x through 4.10 and 5.x through 5.0.1.
insight: Due to an integer overow bug Squid is vulnerable to credential replay and remote code execution attacks against HTTP Digest Authentication tokens.

Name: Squid Security Update Advisory SQUID-2018:3
description: Squid is prone to a denial of service vulnerability due to incorrect buer management when processing HTTP Digest Authentication credentials.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Squid Security Update Advisory SQUID-2018:3
impact: Aected Software/OS Squid versions 3.3.9 - 3.5.28 and 4.x - 4.7.
insight: Due to incorrect input validation the HTTP Request header parser for Digest authentication may access memory outside the allocated memory buer.
On systems with memory access protections this can result in the Squid process being terminated unexpectedly. Resulting in a denial of service for all clients using the proxy.

Name: Squid Buer Overow Vulnerability (GHSA-w4gv-vw3f-29g3, SQUID-2025:1)
description: Squid is prone to a buer overow vulnerability in URN handling.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
impact: Squid prior to version 6.4.

Name: Squid Buffer Overflow Vulnerability (GHSA-w4gv-vw3f-29g3, SQUID-2025:1)
description: Squid is prone to a buffer overflow vulnerability in URN handling.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Squid Buffer Overflow Vulnerability (GHSA-w4gv-vw3f-29g3, SQUID-2025:1) OID:1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.155085 Version used: 2025-08-27T05:39:13Z
impact: Due to incorrect buffer management Squid is vulnerable to a heap buffer overflow and possible remote code execution attack when processing URN.

Name: Squid Multiple Security Update Advisories (SQUID-2019:7, SQUID-2019:8, SQUID-2019:10)
description: Squid is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Squid Multiple Security Update Advisories (SQUID-2019:7, SQUID-2019:8, SQUID-20.
insight: The following aws exist:
- CVE-2019-12526: Heap Overow issue in URN processing
- CVE-2019-18678, CVE-2019-18676: Multiple issues in URI processing
- CVE-2019-18678: HTTP Request Splitting issue in HTTP message processing

Name: Squid Security Update Advisory (SQUID-2019:12)
description: Squid is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
solution: Squid versions 3.x through 3.5.28, 4.x through 4.10 and 5.x through 5.0.1.
insight: The following aws exist:
- CVE-2019-12519: Stack based buer overow vulnerability
- CVE-2019-12521: Heap buer overow vulnerability

Name: Squid 3.5.18 - 3.5.28 / 4.0.10 - 4.7 Multiple Vulnerabilities (SQUID-2019:4)
description: Squid is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: A remote client can:
- deliver crafted URLs to bypass cache manager security controls and retrieve condential details about the proxy and trac it is handling.

- deliver crafted URLs which cause arbitrary content from one origin server to be stored in cache as URLs within another origin. This opens a window of opportunity for clients to be tricked into fetching and XSS execution of that content via side channels.
solution: Note: All Squid-4.x up to and including 4.7 without HTTPS support are NOT vulnerable.
insight: Due to incorrect URL handling Squid is vulnerable to access control bypass, cache poisoning and cross-site scripting attacks when processing HTTP Request messages.

Name: Squid Security Update Advisory (SQUID-2020:7)
description: Squid is prone to a cache poisoning vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Aected Software/OS Squid versions 2.0 through 2.STABLE9, 3.0 through 3.5.28, 4.0 through 4.11 and 5.0.1 through 5.0.2.
insight: Due to incorrect input validation Squid is vulnerable to a Request Smuggling and Poisoning attack against the HTTP cache.

Name: Squid 2.0 < 4.14, 5.0.1 < 5.0.5 HTTP Request Smuggling Vulnerability
description: Squid is prone to an HTTP request smuggling vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Aected Software/OS Squid version 2.0 through 4.13 and 5.0.1 through 5.0.4.
solution: Update to version 4.13, 5.0.5 or later. See the referenced vendor advisory for a workaround.
insight: Due to improper input validation, it allows a trusted client to perform HTTP Request Smuggling and access services otherwise forbidden by the security controls. This occurs for certain uri_whitespace conguration settings.

Name: Squid Buer Overow Vulnerability (SQUID-2022:2)
description: Squid is prone to a buer overow vulnerability in SSPI and SMB authentication.

Name: Squid Buffer Overflow Vulnerability (SQUID-2022:2)
description: Squid is prone to a buffer overflow vulnerability in SSPI and SMB authentication.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: This problem allows a remote client to perform a denial of service attack when Squid is configured to use NTLM or Negotiate authentication with one of the vulnerable helpers.
This problem allows a remote client to extract sensitive information from machine memory when Squid is configured to use NTLM or Negotiate authentication with one of the vulnerable helpers.
The scope of this information includes user credentials in decrypted forms, and also arbitrary memory areas beyond Squid and the helper itself.
This attack is limited to authentication helpers built using the libntlmauth library shipped by Squid.
insight: Due to an incorrect integer overflow protection Squid SSPI and SMB authentication helpers are vulnerable to a buffer overflow attack.

Name: Squid Multiple 0-Day Vulnerabilities (Oct 2023)
description: Squid is prone to multiple zero-day (0-day) vulnerabilities.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 70%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: As of 10/2024 the situation about the versions aected by the previous listed vulnerabilities is largely unclear (The security researcher only stated that all vulnerabilities were discovered in squid-5.0.5 and the vendor only published a few advisories so far).
Due to this unclear situation all Squid versions are currently assumed to be vulnerable by the not yet xed aws.
solution: No known solution was made available for at least one year since the disclosure of this vulnerability. Likely none will be provided anymore. General solution options are to upgrade to a newer release, disable respective features, remove the product or replace the product by another one.

- It seems that some of the aws could be mitigated by workarounds (listed in the referenced GitHub Gist) via either conguration changes and/or by disabling some features / functionality of Squid during build time
- If only these workarounds have been applied and the risk is accepted that these workarounds might not fully mitigate the relevant aw(s) please create an override for this result
insight: The following aws have been reported in 2021 to the vendor and seems to be not xed yet:
- One-Byte Buer OverRead in HTTP Request Header Parsing
- strlen(NULL) Crash Using Digest Authentication GHSA-254c-93q9-cp53
- Gopher Assertion Crash
- Whois Assertion Crash
- RFC 2141 / 2169 (URN) Assertion Crash
- Assertion in Negotiate/NTLM Authentication Using Pipeline Prefetching
- Assertion on IPv6 Host Requests with disable-ipv6
- Assertion Crash on Unexpected 'HTTP/1.1 100 Continue' Response Header
- Pipeline Prefetch Assertion With Double 'Expect:100-continue' Request Headers
- Pipeline Prefetch Assertion With Invalid Headers
- Assertion Crash in Deferred Requests
- Assertion in Digest Authentication
- FTP Authentication Crash
- Assertion Crash In HTTP Response Headers Handling
- Implicit Assertion in Stream Handling
Note: One GHSA advisory has been provided by the security researcher but is not published / available yet.

Name: Squid DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-jm7h-w5q5-jpq9, SQUID-2020:13)
description: Squid is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
solution: As a workaround reject all gopher URL requests. Please see the referenced vendor advisory for more information.
insight: This problem allows a remote gopher: server to trigger a buer overow by delivering large gopher protocol responses. On most operating systems with memory protection this will halt Squid service immediately, causing a denial of service to all Squid clients.
The gopher protocol is always available and enabled in Squid prior to Squid 6.0.1.

Name: Squid Multiple DoS Vulnerabilities (GHSA-f975-v7qw-q7hj, SQUID-2024:4)
description: Squid is prone to multiple denial of service (DoS) vulnerabilities due to multiple issues in ESI.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Squid Multiple DoS Vulnerabilities (GHSA-f975-v7qw-q7hj, SQUID-2024:4) OID:1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.114851
impact: Squid version 3.0 through 6.x.
insight: Due to Input Validation, Premature Release of Resource During Expected Lifetime, and Missing Release of Resource after Eective Lifetime bugs, Squid is vulnerable to Denial of Service attacks by a trusted server against all clients using the proxy.
These aws were part of the 'Squid Caching Proxy Security Audit: 55 vulnerabilities and 35 0days' publication in October 2023 and led as:
- Memory Leak in ESI Error Processing
- Assertion in ESI Header Handling
- Use-After-Free in ESI 'Try' (and 'Choose') Processing
- Use-After-Free in ESI Expression Evaluation
- Assertion Due to 0 ESI 'when' Checking
- Assertion Using ESI's When Directive
- Assertion in ESI Variable Assignment (String)
- Assertion in ESI Variable Assignment
- Null Pointer Dereference In ESI's esi:include and esi:when

Name: Squid Multiple DoS Vulnerabilities (GHSA-543m-w2m2-g255, SQUID-2023:2)
description: Squid is prone to multiple denial of service (DoS) vulnerabilities.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Squid Multiple DoS Vulnerabilities (GHSA-543m-w2m2-g255, SQUID-2023:2) OID:1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.100705
insight: The following aws exist:   - Due to an Improper Handling of Structural Elements bug Squid is vulnerable to a Denial of  Service attack against HTTP and HTTPS clients.   - Due to an Incomplete Filtering of Special Elements bug Squid is vulnerable to a Denial of  Service attack against HTTP and HTTPS clients.   These aws were part of the 'Squid Caching Proxy Security Audit: 55 vulnerabilities and 35  0days' publication in October 2023 and led as 'Cache Poisoning by Large Stored Response  Headers (With Bonus XSS)'.

Name: Squid Security Update Advisory SQUID-2020:6
description: Squid is prone to a denial of service vulnerability in the TLS handshake.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Squid Security Update Advisory SQUID-2020:6
insight: Due to use of a potentially dangerous function Squid and the default certicate validation helper are vulnerable to a denial of service attack when processing TLS certicates.

Name: Squid Security Update Advisory SQUID-2020:9
description: Squid is prone to a denial of service vulnerability when processing Cache Digest responses.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Squid Security Update Advisory SQUID-2020:9
impact: This problem allows a trusted peer to perform a Denial of Service by consuming all available CPU cycles on the machine running Squid when handling a crafted Cache Digest response message.
This attack is limited to Squid using cache_peer with cache digests feature.
insight: Due to Improper Input Validation Squid is vulnerable to a denial of service attack against the machine operating Squid.

Name: Squid Information Disclosure Vulnerability (GHSA-c8cc-phh7-xmxr, SQUID-2025:2)
description: Squid is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Squid Information Disclosure Vulnerability (GHSA-c8cc-phh7-xmxr, SQUID-2025:2)
insight: Due to a failure to redact HTTP Authentication credentials Squid is vulnerable to an information disclosure attack.

Name: Squid DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-xggx-9329-3c27, SQUID-2023:8)
description: Squid is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Due to an Incorrect Check of Function Return Value bug Squid is vulnerable to a Denial of Service attack against its Helper process management.
This aw was part of the 'Squid Caching Proxy Security Audit: 55 vulnerabilities and 35 0days' publication in October 2023 and led as 'Assertion in Squid Helper Process Creator'.
insight: Due to an Incorrect Check of Function Return Value bug Squid is vulnerable to a Denial of Service attack against its Helper process management.
This aw was part of the 'Squid Caching Proxy Security Audit: 55 vulnerabilities and 35 0days' publication in October 2023 and led as 'Assertion in Squid Helper Process Creator'.

Name: Squid DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-wgq4-4cfg-c4x3, SQUID-2023:10)
description: Squid is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Due to an uncontrolled recursion bug, Squid may be vulnerable to denial of service attack against HTTP request parsing.
This aw was part of the 'Squid Caching Proxy Security Audit: 55 vulnerabilities and 35 0days' publication in October 2023 and led as 'X-Forwarded-For Stack Overow'.
insight: Due to an uncontrolled recursion bug, Squid may be vulnerable to denial of service attack against HTTP request parsing.
This aw was part of the 'Squid Caching Proxy Security Audit: 55 vulnerabilities and 35 0days' publication in October 2023 and led as 'X-Forwarded-For Stack Overow'.

Name: Squid DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-phqj-m8gv-cq4g, SQUID-2023:3)
description: Squid is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
insight: Due to a buer overow bug Squid is vulnerable to a Denial of Service attack against HTTP Digest Authentication.
This aw was part of the 'Squid Caching Proxy Security Audit: 55 vulnerabilities and 35 0days' publication in October 2023 and led as 'Buer Overow in Digest Authentication'.

Name: Squid DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-rj5h-46j6-q2g5, SQUID-2023:9)
description: Squid is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
insight: Due to a Use-After-Free bug Squid is vulnerable to a Denial of Service attack against collapsed forwarding.
This aw was part of the 'Squid Caching Proxy Security Audit: 55 vulnerabilities and 35 0days' publication in October 2023 and led as 'Use-After-Free in TRACE Requests'.

Name: Squid DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-h5x6-w8mv-xfpr, SQUID-2024:2)
description: Squid is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Aected Software/OS
Squid versions prior to 6.5.
insight: Due to a Collapse of Data into Unsafe Value bug, Squid may be vulnerable to a Denial of Service attack against HTTP header parsing.
This aw was part of the 'Squid Caching Proxy Security Audit: 55 vulnerabilities and 35 0days' publication in October 2023 and led as 'Memory Leak in HTTP Response Parsing'.

Name: Squid DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-cg5h-v6vc-w33f, SQUID-2021:8)
description: Squid is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability in the Gopher gateway.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Aected Software/OS
Squid version 2.x and later prior to version 6.0.1.
solution: As a workaround reject all gopher URL requests. Please see the referenced vendor advisory for more information.
Note: Removing the gopher port 70 from the Safe_ports ACL is not sucient to avoid this vulnerability.
insight: Due to a NULL pointer dereference bug Squid is vulnerable to a Denial of Service attack against Squid's Gopher gateway.
The gopher protocol is always available and enabled in Squid prior to Squid 6.0.1.
Responses triggering this bug are possible to be received from any gopher server, even those without malicious intent.
This aw was part of the 'Squid Caching Proxy Security Audit: 55 vulnerabilities and 35 0days' publication in October 2023 and led as 'Null Pointer Dereference in Gopher Response Handling'.

Name: Squid DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-72c2-c3wm-8qxc, SQUID-2024:1)
description: Squid is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability in the HTTP Chunked Decoding.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
insight: Due to an Uncontrolled Recursion bug, Squid may be vulnerable to a Denial of Service attack against HTTP Chunked decoder.
This aw was part of the 'Squid Caching Proxy Security Audit: 55 vulnerabilities and 35 0days' publication in October 2023 and led as 'Chunked Encoding Stack Overow'.

Name: Squid DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-73m6-jm96-c6r3, SQUID-2023:4)
description: Squid is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability in the SSL Certicate validation.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
insight: Due to an Improper Validation of Specied Index bug Squid is vulnerable to a Denial of Service attack against SSL Certicate validation.
This aw was part of the 'Squid Caching Proxy Security Audit: 55 vulnerabilities and 35 0days' publication in October 2023 and led as 'Buer UnderRead in SSL CN Parsing'.

description: Squid is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.

Name: Squid Multiple Security Update Advisories (SQUID-2019:9, SQUID-2019:11)
description: Squid is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.

Name: Squid 2.0 < 4.14, 5.0.1 < 5.0.5 DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-ch36-9jhx-phm4, SQUID-2021:1)
description: Squid is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability in the URN processing.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.

Details: Squid 2.0 < 4.14, 5.0.1 < 5.0.5 DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-ch36-9jhx-phm4, SQUID-2. ->.. OID:1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.146030 Version used: 2024-11-01T05:05:36Z Product Detection Result Product: cpe:/a:squid-cache:squid:3.5.27 Method: Squid Detection (HTTP) OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.900611)
impact: Aected Software/OS Squid version 2.0 through 4.14 and 5.0.1 through 5.0.5.
Vulnerability Insight Due to a buer management bug Squid is vulnerable to a DoS attack against the server it is operating on.
This attack is limited to proxies which attempt to resolve a 'urn:' resource identier. Support for this resolving is enabled by default in all Squid.
This problem allows a malicious server in collaboration with a trusted client to consume arbitrarily large amounts of memory on the server running Squid.
Lack of available memory resources impacts all services on the machine running Squid. Once initiated the DoS situation will persist until Squid is shutdown.
This aw was part of the 'Squid Caching Proxy Security Audit: 55 vulnerabilities and 35 0days' publication in October 2023 and led as 'RFC 2141 / 2169 (URN) Response Parsing Memory Leak'.
solution: Update to version 4.15, 5.0.6 or later. See the referenced vendor advisory for a workaround.

Name: Squid Security Update Advisory (SQUID-2018:1)
description: Squid is vulnerable to denial of service attack when processing ESI responses.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: This problem allows a remote server delivering certain ESI response syntax to trigger a denial of service for all clients accessing the Squid service.
solution: Update to version 4.0.23 or later. Patches are available, please see the references for details.
insight: Due to unrelated changes Squid-3.5 has become vulnerable to some regular ESI server responses also triggering this issue. This problem is limited to the Squid custom ESI parser.

Name: Squid Security Update Advisory (SQUID-2018:2)
description: Squid is vulnerable to denial of service (DoS) attack when processing ESI responses.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: This problem allows a remote server delivering certain ESI response syntax to trigger a denial of service for all clients accessing the Squid service.
insight: Due to incorrect pointer handling Squid is vulnerable to denial of service attack when processing ESI responses or downloading intermediate CA certicates.

Name: Squid 2.0 - 4.17, 5.0.1 < 5.6 DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-f5cp-6rh3-284w, SQUID-2021:7)
description: Squid is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.

Name: Squid 2.5.STABLE2 < 4.15, 5.0.1 < 5.0.6 Multiple DoS Vulnerabilities (GHSA-pxwq-f3qr-w2xf, SQUID-2021:4)
description: Squid is prone to multiple denial of service (DoS) vulnerabilities.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
solution: Squid version 2.5.STABLE2 through 2.7.STABLE9, 3.0 through 4.14 and 5.0.1 through 5.0.5.
insight: The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2021-31806: Due to an incorrect input validation bug Squid is vulnerable to a DoS attack against all clients using the proxy.
- CVE-2021-31807: Due to an incorrect memory management bug Squid is vulnerable to a DoS attack against all clients using the proxy.
- CVE-2021-31808: Due to an integer overow bug Squid is vulnerable to a DoS attack against all clients using the proxy.

These aws were part of the 'Squid Caching Proxy Security Audit: 55 vulnerabilities and 35 0days' publication in October 2023 and led as 'Partial Content Parsing Use-After-Free', 'Integer Overow in Range Header' and 'Unsatisable Range Requests Assertion'.

Name: Squid < 4.15, 5.0.x < 5.0.6 DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-572g-rvwr-6c7f, SQUID-2021:5)
description: Squid is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Squid versions prior to 4.15 and 5.0.0 through 5.0.5.
Due to an input validation bug Squid is vulnerable to a DoS against all clients using the proxy.
This problem allows a remote server to perform a DoS when delivering HTTP Response messages.
The issue trigger is a header which can be expected to exist in HTTP trac without any malicious
intent by the server.
This aw was part of the 'Squid Caching Proxy Security Audit: 55 vulnerabilities and 35 0days'
publication in October 2023 and led as 'Crash in Content-Range Response Header Logic'.
insight: Due to an input validation bug Squid is vulnerable to a DoS against all clients using the proxy.
This problem allows a remote server to perform a DoS when delivering HTTP Response messages.
The issue trigger is a header which can be expected to exist in HTTP trac without any malicious
intent by the server.
This aw was part of the 'Squid Caching Proxy Security Audit: 55 vulnerabilities and 35 0days'
publication in October 2023 and led as 'Crash in Content-Range Response Header Logic'.

Name: Squid DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-j49p-553x-48rx, SQUID-2023:11)
description: Squid is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%

Name: Squid Security Update Advisory (SQUID-2018:4)
description: Squid is prone to a cross-site scripting vulnerability to incorrect input handling when generating HTTPS response messages about TLS errors.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: This problem allows a malicious HTTPS server to trigger error page delivery to a client and also inject arbitrary HTML code into the resulting error response.
This problem is limited to Squid built with TLS / SSL support.
insight: Aected Software/OS Squid proxy versions 3.1.12.1 through 3.1.23, 3.2.0.4 through 3.5.28 and 4.x through 4.3.

Name: Squid Security Update Advisory (SQUID-2019:6)
description: Squid is prone to multiple cross-site scripting vulnerabilities due to incorrect input handling in the cachemgr.cgi tool.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: This allows a malicious server to embed URLs in its content such that user credentials and other information can be extracted from a client or administrator with access to the Squid cachemgr.cgi tool URL.
insight: Aected Software/OS Squid proxy versions 2.x, 3.x through 3.5.28 and 4.x through 4.7.

Name: Squid < 7.0.1 XSS Vulnerability (SQUID-2023:6)
description: Squid is prone to a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in cachemgr.cgi.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Squid versions prior to version 7.0.1.
insight: Squid, when certain web browsers are used, mishandles HTML in the host (aka hostname) parameter to cachemgr.cgi.
Note: This aw had received an incomplete x in version 4.9 back in 2019.

Name: Squid Security Update Advisory SQUID-2019:2
description: Squid is prone to a denial of service vulnerability due to incorrect buer management when processing HTTP Basic Authentication credentials.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Squid Security Update Advisory SQUID-2019:2
impact: Squid versions 2.x - 2.7.STABLE9, 3.x - 3.5.28 and 4.x - 4.7.
insight: Due to incorrect string termination the Basic authentication credentials decoder may access memory outside the decode buer.
On systems with memory access protections this can result in the Squid process being terminated unexpectedly. Resulting in a denial of service for all clients using the proxy.

Name: Squid DoS Vulnerability (SQUID-2018:3)
description: Squid is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Successful exploitation will allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service.
solution: A patch and workaround is also available. Please see the references for more information.
insight: The aw exists due to incorrect pointer handling.

Name: Squid Security Update Advisory SQUID-2018:5
description: Squid is prone to a denial of service vulnerability due to a memory leak in the SNMP query rejection code.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Squid Security Update Advisory SQUID-2018:5
insight: This problem allows a remote attacker to consume all memory available to the Squid process, causing it to crash.

In environments where per-process memory restrictions are not enforced strictly, or congured to large values this may also aect other processes operating on the same machine. Leading to a much worse denial of service situation.
This problem is limited to Squid built with SNMP support and receiving SNMP trac.

Name: Squid Information Disclosure Vulnerability (SQUID-2020:12)
description: Squid is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Successful exploitation would allow an attacker to read sensitive information.
solution: Squid proxy version 2.6 through 2.7.STABLE9, 3.x through 3.5.28, 4.x through 4.16 and 5.x through 5.1.
insight: The vulnerability exists because of an out-of-bounds read in WCCP protocol data.

Name: Squid Request/Response Smuggling Vulnerability (GHSA-j83v-w3p4-5cqh, SQUID-2023:1)
description: Squid is prone to a request/response smuggling vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Squid Request/Response Smuggling Vulnerability (GHSA-j83v-w3p4-5cqh, SQUID-2023.
impact: Squid versions 2.6 through 6.3.
insight: Due to chunked decoder lenience Squid is vulnerable to Request/Response smuggling attacks when parsing HTTP/1.1 and ICAP messages.

Name: Squid DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-wgvf-q977-9xjg, SQUID-2024:3)
description: Squid is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability in ESI processing.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Squid version 3.0 through 6.9.
insight: Due to an Out-of-bounds Write error when assigning ESI variables, Squid is susceptible to a Memory Corruption error, which can result in a Denial of Service.
This aw was part of the 'Squid Caching Proxy Security Audit: 55 vulnerabilities and 35 0days' publication in October 2023 and led as 'Buer Underow in ESI'.

Name: Squid 1.0 < 4.14, 5.0 < 5.0.5 DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-m47m-9hvw-7447, SQUID-2021:3)
description: Squid is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability in the Cache Manager.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
impact: Squid version 1.0 through 4.14 and 5.0 through 5.0.5.
solution: Update to version 4.15, 5.0.6 or later. See the referenced vendor advisory for a workaround.

Name: Squid <= 4.14 Privilege Escalation Vulnerability
description: Squid is prone to a privilege escalation vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: This behaviour makes it trivial for an attacker who has compromised the child process to escalate their privileges back to root.
solution: No known solution was made available for at least one year since the disclosure of this vulnerability. Likely none will be provided anymore. General solution options are to upgrade to a newer release, disable respective features, remove the product or replace the product by another one.
insight: When Squid is run as root, it spawns its child processes as a lesser user, by default the user nobody. This is done via the leave_suid call. leave_suid leaves the Saved UID as 0.

Name: Squid Buffer Overflow Vulnerability (Sep 2025)
description: Squid is prone to a buffer overflow vulnerability as it mishandles ASN.1 encoding of long SNMP OIDs.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: A stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the SNMP (Simple Network Management Protocol) message processing component of Squid Cache. The flaw is present in the asn_build_objid function located in the lib/snmplib/asn1.c library, which is responsible for encoding Object Identifiers (OIDs) into the ASN.1 BER format for SNMP responses.
solution: No known solution is available as of 30th September, 2025. Information regarding this issue will be updated once solution details are available.
insight: Affected Software/OS Squid version 7.1 and prior.

Name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
description: The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.
detection_result: It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between: Packet 1: 776668943 Packet 2: 776670063
impact: A side effect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.
solution: To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.

To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled' Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled. The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.

Name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
description: The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between:
detection_method: Special IP packets are forged and sent with a little delay in between to the target IP. The responses are searched for a timestamps. If found, the timestamps are reported.
impact: A side eect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.
solution: To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.

The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.
insight: The remote host implements TCP timestamps, as dened by RFC1323/RFC7323.

Name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
description: The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: The following response / ICMP packet has been received:
impact: This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number gener- ators in other services.

Name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
description: The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.
detection_result: The following response / ICMP packet has been received:
detection_method: Sends an ICMP Timestamp (Type 13) request and checks if a Timestamp Reply (Type 14) is received.
impact: This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.
solution: Solution type: Mitigation   Various mitigations are possible:   - Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely  - Protect the remote host by a rewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the rewall in  either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)
insight: The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.

Name: Web Application Scanning Consolidation / Info Reporting
description: The script consolidates and reports various information for web application (formerly called 'CGI') scanning.
This information is based on the following scripts / settings:
- HTTP-Version Detection (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.100034)
- Web mirroring / webmirror.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10662)
- Directory Scanner / DDI_Directory_Scanner.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.11032)
- The congured 'cgi_path' within the 'Scanner Preferences' of the scan cong in use

- The congured 'Enable CGI scanning', 'Enable generic web application scanning' and 'Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning' within the 'Global variable settings' of the scan cong in use
If you think any of this information is wrong please report it to the referenced community forum.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: The Hostname/IP "squid35.trabalho_vulnnet" was used to access the remote host.
Generic web application scanning is disabled for this host via the "Enable generic web application scanning" option within the "Global variable settings" of the scan config in use.
Requests to this service are done via HTTP/1.1.
This service seems to be able to host PHP scripts.
This service seems to be able to host ASP scripts.
The User-Agent "Mozilla/5.0 [en] (X11, U; OpenVAS-VT <version replaced>)" was used to access the remote host.

Historic /scripts and /cgi-bin are not added to the directories used for web application scanning. You can enable this again with the "Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning" option within the "Global variable settings" of the scan config in use.
The following directories were used for web application scanning:
While this is not, in and of itself, a bug, you should manually inspect these directories to ensure that they are in compliance with company security standards
log_method: Details: Web Application Scanning Consolidation / Info Reporting

Name: Traceroute
description: Collect information about the network route and network distance between the scanner host and the target host.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: Network route from scanner (172.30.0.2) to target (172.31.1.79):
Network distance between scanner and target: 2
insight: For internal networks, the distances are usually small, often less than 4 hosts between scanner and target. For public targets the distance is greater and might be 10 hosts or more.
log_method: A combination of the protocols ICMP and TCP is used to determine the route. This method is applicable for IPv4 only and it is also known as 'traceroute'.

Name: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check
description: Checks if the remote host has IP forwarding enabled.
detection_result: It was possible to route a TCP packet through the target host and received an an ->swer which means IP forwarding is enabled.
log_method: Sends a crafted Local Link Layer (LLL) frame and checks the response.

Name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting
description: This script consolidates the OS information detected by several VTs and tries to nd the best matching OS.
Furthermore it reports all previously collected information leading to this best matching OS. It also reports possible additional information which might help to improve the OS detection.
If any of this information is wrong or could be improved please consider to report these to the referenced community forum.
detection_result: Concluded from ICMP based OS fingerprint
Setting key "Host/runs_unixoide" based on this information

Name: Hostname Determination Reporting
description: The script reports information on how the hostname of the target was determined.

Name: CPE Inventory
description: This routine uses information collected by other routines about CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.

Note: Some CPEs for specic products might show up twice or more in the output. Background: After a product got renamed or a specic vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older CPE.
